items.q: How long does the ATP-PC system last at maximum intensity?
items.why: Students overestimate the ATP-PC system. It provides instant energy but stores deplete in ~10 seconds (one sprint, one heavy lift). After that, glycolysis or aerobic systems take over.

items.correct: Information the performer senses from their own body during/after movement.
items.why: Students reverse the meaning. Intrinsic = from WITHIN (kinesthetic awareness, feel); extrinsic = from OUTSIDE (coach feedback, video, statistics). Experienced performers rely more on intrinsic feedback.
Students confuse storage with circulation. Blood carries glucose; muscles and liver store glycogen (the storage form). Liver glycogen regulates blood sugar; muscle glycogen fuels local contraction.
It's Defending close to the ball. “Standing still” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Being available for a pass. “Cheering” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many big phases of play were named (attack + defence)?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
